dose: 1000 mg IV every 2 weeks (optimal biological dose)
tam_definition: CSF1R+ macrophages and CD68/CD163+ macrophages by IHC
change_magnitude: 'Substantial decrease' in CSF1R+ and CD68/CD163+ macrophages in on-treatment biopsies (36 patients with paired tissue)
durability_described: 2-year ORR 64%; responses durable during maintained dosing
clinical_correlate: ORR 71%; 2-year ORR 64%; symptomatic/functional improvement (WOMAC, EQ-5D-3L)
notes: Cassier 2020 EJC long-term update of emactuzumab in dTGCT. On-target macrophage depletion in synovial TGCT tissue (disease driven by CSF1/CSF1R axis). Abstract reports qualitative 'substantial decrease' in CSF1R+ and CD68/CD163+ macrophages; full-text would quantify. Landmark example of clinical TAM depletion with durable efficacy.
title: Long-term clinical activity, safety and patient-reported quality of life in patients with tenosynovial giant cell tumour treated with emactuzumab

indication: Advanced solid tumors (ICB-naive UBC, melanoma; ICB-experienced NSCLC, UBC, melanoma)
tam_definition: TAM subpopulations by IHC/mIF
change_magnitude: TAM reduction observed but 'less TAM reduction in ICB-experienced compared with ICB-naïve patients'; persistence of a TAM subpopulation associated with CD8+ TIL increase
clinical_correlate: ICB-naive UBC cORR 9.8%; CD8+ TIL increase associated with TAM subpopulation persistence (interpreted as productive macrophage remodeling rather than ablation)
notes: Gomez-Roca 2022 JITC — emactuzumab + atezo phase 1b. Key observation: TAM reduction is less complete in ICB-experienced patients; a persistent TAM subpopulation correlates with CD8 TIL increase. Interpreted as repolarization signal alongside depletion. Abstract-level; quantitative magnitudes require full-text.
title: Anti-CSF-1R emactuzumab in combination with anti-PD-L1 atezolizumab in advanced solid tumor patients naive or experienced for immune checkpoint blockade

tam_definition: M2 macrophages by IHC
change_magnitude: 'Decreased proportion of activated M2 macrophages within tumor samples with treatment'
clinical_correlate: Clinical benefit 12/18 (67%): 3 PR + 9 SD
notes: Manji 2021 Clin Cancer Res — pexidartinib + sirolimus in sarcoma. Qualitative M2 macrophage reduction in tumor IHC; no absolute count values in abstract.
title: A Phase I Study of the Combination of Pexidartinib and Sirolimus to Target Tumor-Associated Macrophages in Unresectable Sarcoma and Malignant Peripheral Nerve Sheath Tumors

tissue_detail: Peripheral blood CD14dim/CD16+ monocytes (pre-specified as CSF1R-depletion PD biomarker)
tam_definition: CD14dim/CD16+ non-classical monocytes (CSF1-dependent; TAM-precursor surrogate per pre-specification)
change_magnitude: CD14dim/CD16+ monocyte levels decreased by 57-100%; plasma CSF-1 increased 1.6- to 53-fold
notes: Wesolowski 2019 Ther Adv Med Oncol — pexidartinib + paclitaxel phase 1b. Blood CSF1-dependent CD14dim/CD16+ monocytes pre-specified as CSF1R-depletion PD surrogate (D1 satisfied). Dose-dependent CSF1 feedback rise. No tumor TAM data in abstract.
title: Phase Ib study of the combination of pexidartinib (PLX3397), a CSF-1R inhibitor, and paclitaxel in patients with advanced solid tumors

indication: Metastatic breast cancer (MBC) and castration-resistant prostate cancer (mCRPC)
tissue_detail: Blood CD14dim CD16bright non-classical monocytes (pre-specified CSF1R-depletion surrogate)
tam_definition: CD14dim CD16bright non-classical monocytes (pre-specified TAM-precursor surrogate)
change_magnitude: CD14dim CD16bright monocytes decreased at day 8; circulating CSF-1 increased
clinical_correlate: 2 MBC patients SD >9 months; otherwise limited clinical activity
notes: Autio 2020 Clin Cancer Res — LY3022855 monotherapy. Blood CD14dim CD16bright monocyte pre-specified PD (D1 satisfied). No tumor CD68 quantification in abstract.
title: Immunomodulatory Activity of a Colony-stimulating Factor-1 Receptor Inhibitor in Patients with Advanced Refractory Breast or Prostate Cancer

tam_definition: Skin macrophages by IHC
change_magnitude: 'Reduced numbers of skin macrophages' plus pharmacodynamic serum CSF1 increase
clinical_correlate: SD 32% (8/25); no objective responses
notes: Papadopoulos 2017 Clin Cancer Res — AMG 820 first-in-human. Skin used as tissue-macrophage PD surrogate (biopsy of accessible skin, not tumor). Serum CSF1 increase as feedback. No tumor TAM data.
title: First-in-Human Study of AMG 820, a Monoclonal Anti-Colony-Stimulating Factor 1 Receptor Antibody, in Patients with Advanced Solid Tumors

dose: AMG 820 1100 mg or 1400 mg IV + pembrolizumab 200 mg Q3W
tissue_detail: Blood CD16-expressing monocytes (pre-specified CSF1-dependent PD surrogate)
tam_definition: CSF1-dependent CD16-expressing monocytes
change_magnitude: Reduction in CSF1-dependent CD16-expressing monocytes; accumulation of serum CSF1 and IL-34
clinical_correlate: Insufficient antitumor activity for further evaluation
notes: Razak 2020 JITC — AMG 820 + pembrolizumab. PD target engagement (CD16+ monocyte reduction, CSF1/IL-34 feedback) achieved but not translated to clinical benefit — a PD/efficacy disconnect.
title: Safety and efficacy of AMG 820, an anti-colony-stimulating factor 1 receptor antibody, in combination with pembrolizumab in patients with advanced solid tumors

tam_definition: Tumor immune infiltrate by IHC (PD-L1, CD4, CD8)
change_magnitude: Increased PD-L1 expression and increased CD4 and CD8 cells in tumor biopsies
notes: Same trial as above (Razak 2020). Tumor compartment data is T-cell/PD-L1 centric; direct tumor CD68/CD163 TAM counts not reported. Row retained as paired PD endpoint from the same trial, with readout_type: other and depletion_success: not-assessed since no direct TAM quantification.
title: Safety and efficacy of AMG 820, an anti-colony-stimulating factor 1 receptor antibody, in combination with pembrolizumab in patients with advanced solid tumors

tam_definition: CD14dim CD16bright non-classical monocytes
change_magnitude: TAMs and CD14dim CD16bright monocyte levels decreased with treatment; serum CSF-1 and IL-34 increased
clinical_correlate: Limited clinical activity (3 SD, no responses)
notes: Dowlati 2021 Invest New Drugs — LY3022855 monotherapy phase 1. Abstract reports TAMs (unclear whether tumor or blood monocyte surrogate) decreased; PD engagement achieved without clinical efficacy.
title: LY3022855, an anti-colony stimulating factor-1 receptor (CSF-1R) monoclonal antibody, in patients with advanced solid tumors

dose: LY3022855 100 mg QW + durvalumab 750 mg Q2W
tam_definition: Pharmacokinetic + PK/PD markers (not specified in abstract)
notes: Falchook 2021 Invest New Drugs — LY3022855 + durvalumab/tremelimumab phase 1a/1b. Abstract is PK-focused with no explicit macrophage/monocyte PD data. Retained as companion row to Dowlati 2021 for intervention-class coverage; no specific TAM PD readout reported in abstract.
title: A phase 1a/1b trial of CSF-1R inhibitor LY3022855 in combination with durvalumab or tremelimumab in patients with advanced solid tumors

tissue_detail: Blood CD14dim CD16bright non-classical monocytes (pre-specified CSF1R-depletion PD surrogate)
tam_definition: CD14dim CD16bright non-classical monocytes
change_magnitude: Reduction of CD14dim CD16bright monocytes; increase of Ki67+ CD8+ T cells; decrease of B cells
clinical_correlate: Best objective clinical response: SD in 40.5%; no ORs
notes: Machiels 2020 JITC — emactuzumab + selicrelumab. Blood CD14dim CD16bright monocytes are pre-specified CSF1R-depletion PD surrogate (D1 satisfied). N=37 is estimate from reported 40.5% SD rate — actual N to verify in full text.
title: Phase Ib study of anti-CSF-1R antibody emactuzumab in combination with CD40 agonist selicrelumab in advanced solid tumor patients

combo_partners: APX005M (CD40 agonist) + nivolumab (anti-PD-1); cabiralizumab is anti-CSF1R
dose: APX005M 0.3 mg/kg + cabiralizumab 4 mg/kg + nivolumab 240 mg Q2W
tissue_detail: Serum cytokines and peripheral immune cells (pre-specified)
tam_definition: CD40 and MCSF serum markers (surrogate for macrophage activation)
change_magnitude: Pro-inflammatory cytokine upregulation at 4h; CD40 and MCSF serum increase post-therapy
notes: Weiss 2021 Clin Cancer Res — dual macrophage-polarizing therapy (CD40 agonist + CSF1Ri) + nivo in ICI-resistant disease. PD engagement but poor clinical activity. Macrophage-polarization surrogates captured; no direct tumor TAM count readout in abstract.
title: A Phase I Study of APX005M and Cabiralizumab with or without Nivolumab in Patients with Melanoma, Kidney Cancer, or Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer Resistant to Anti-PD-1/PD-L1

patient_population: cGVHD after allogeneic HCT, prior systemic therapy failure
tam_definition: CSF1R-dependent monocyte-macrophage lineage (pre-specified PD target; cGVHD driven by CSF1R+ donor-derived macrophages)
change_magnitude: ORR 50% at C7D1 (primary endpoint); ORR 82% across first 6 cycles
durability_described: Durable clinical responses; 58% significant symptom improvement per Lee Symptom Scale
clinical_correlate: Lee Symptom Scale improvement in 58%; led to FDA approval Aug 2024
notes: Kitko 2023 JCO — axatilimab in cGVHD phase 1/2. Not a cancer indication but the best clinical proof of CSF1R-dependent macrophage-driven disease modulation in humans. Kept as in-scope because CSF1R macrophage ablation is the mechanistic target. No direct tumor TAM counts (not applicable); clinical-response magnitude used as PD surrogate.
title: Axatilimab for Chronic Graft-Versus-Host Disease after failure of at least two prior lines of systemic therapy: Results of a Phase I/II study

indication: Relapsed/refractory pediatric leukemias and solid tumors
tissue_detail: Plasma CSF-1 + absolute monocyte count (pre-specified CSF1R-engagement PD markers)
tam_definition: Absolute monocyte count + plasma MCSF (pre-specified PD surrogate for CSF1R target engagement)
change_magnitude: Plasma MCSF rise; absolute monocyte count decrease
durability_described: One mesothelioma patient SD at C49+
clinical_correlate: One patient 67% RECIST reduction; 2 SD
notes: Boal 2020 Clin Cancer Res — pediatric pexidartinib phase 1. Blood monocyte count + MCSF as pre-specified CSF1R PD surrogate.
title: Pediatric PK/PD Phase I Trial of Pexidartinib in Relapsed and Refractory Leukemias and Solid Tumors Including Neurofibromatosis Type I-Related Plexiform Neurofibromas

dose: Monotherapy 10-60 mg QD; combination 20-40 mg QD + nivolumab
patient_population: Advanced solid tumors, PD-1/PD-L1-experienced or naive
tissue_detail: Peripheral immune cells and cytokines (pre-specified PD)
tam_definition: Myeloid/Treg/cytokine panel (pre-specified PD)
change_magnitude: PD-activity reported but abstract does not specify TAM/myeloid PD magnitude
clinical_correlate: Antitumor activity observed in combination, including prior PD-1/PD-L1 progressors
notes: Hong 2023 Clin Cancer Res — MARIO-1 eganelisib mono/combo phase 1. Abstract emphasizes safety and clinical activity; no quantitative TAM PD in abstract. MARIO-3 (O'Connell 2024) contains the TAM reprogramming data (captured below).
title: Eganelisib, A First-In-Class PI3Kγ Inhibitor, in Patients with Advanced Solid Tumors: Results of the Phase 1/1b MARIO-1 Trial

dose: Eganelisib 30 mg or 40 mg QD + atezo + nab-pac
tissue_detail: Paired pre/on-treatment tumor biopsies; mIF + GeoMx spatial transcriptomics
tam_definition: TAM reprogramming gene signatures + multiplex IF
change_magnitude: Gene signatures of TAM reprogramming, immune activation, and ECM reorganization detected; associated with longer PFS
clinical_correlate: TAM reprogramming associated with longer PFS in treated patients
notes: O'Connell 2024 JITC — MARIO-3 eganelisib + atezo + nab-pac TNBC. Paired biopsy multiplex IF + GeoMx spatial transcriptomics; TAM reprogramming (M2→M1) is the intended mechanism. Repolarization row; multi-marker evidence supports `succeeded`. N=12 refers to spatial transcriptomics paired-biopsy subset; 18 for PD-L1 IHC subset.
title: Eganelisib combined with immune checkpoint inhibitor therapy and chemotherapy in frontline metastatic triple-negative breast cancer triggers macrophage reprogramming, immune activation and extracellular matrix reorganization

indication: Borderline resectable / locally advanced pancreatic adenocarcinoma
tam_definition: CCR2+ inflammatory monocytes and tumor-associated macrophages
change_magnitude: Reduction in CCR2+ inflammatory monocytes and TAMs in blood and tumor (per paper's PD analysis)
clinical_correlate: Objective response 49% (16/33 evaluable) in combo vs 0% in FOLFIRINOX alone
notes: Nywening 2016 Lancet Oncol — landmark CCR2i+FOLFIRINOX pancreatic. Abstract safety/efficacy focused; full PMC text has monocyte/TAM PD data. Abstract-level extraction — numeric values require PMC full-text backfill.
title: Targeting tumour-associated macrophages with CCR2 inhibition in combination with FOLFIRINOX in patients with borderline resectable and locally advanced pancreatic cancer: a single-centre, open-label, dose-finding, non-randomised, phase 1b trial

tam_definition: CCR2+ classical monocytes (TAM-precursor surrogate per pre-specification)
change_magnitude: No significant change in PD biomarkers; pulmonary toxicity led to study halt
clinical_correlate: ORR 23.8%; study terminated due to pulmonary toxicity
notes: Noel 2020 Invest New Drugs — PF-04136309 + nab-pac/gem phase 1b. Null-result for CCR2-targeting PD in this chemo backbone; contrast with Nywening 2016 FOLFIRINOX success. Pulmonary toxicity and study halt are critical negative context.
title: Phase 1b study of a small molecule antagonist of human chemokine (C-C motif) receptor 2 (PF-04136309) in combination with nab-paclitaxel/gemcitabine in first-line treatment of metastatic pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma

intervention: BMS-813160 (CCR2/5 dual antagonist) + nivolumab + gemcitabine + nab-paclitaxel
indication: Borderline resectable / locally advanced pancreatic cancer
tam_definition: Intratumoral monocytes and macrophages by IHC + transcriptomics
change_magnitude: Intratumoral monocytes and macrophages decreased; T-cell proliferation and effector genes enhanced
clinical_correlate: Borderline resectable ORR 42% vs 0% control; resection 83.3%; mPFS 11.9 mo; mOS 18.2 mo
notes: Grierson 2025 Clin Cancer Res — BMS-813160 + nivo + GnP neoadjuvant PDAC. Randomized-controlled design with GnP-alone control (N=8). Clear intratumoral TAM reduction + T-cell activation + clinical activity.
title: Neoadjuvant BMS-813160 with Nivolumab and Gemcitabine plus Nab-Paclitaxel for Patients with Borderline Resectable or Locally Advanced Pancreatic Cancer

dose: 5F9 1 mg/kg prime + 10-30 mg/kg QW maintenance + rituximab
tam_definition: Macrophage-mediated phagocytosis of CD47+ tumor cells (mechanism-of-action); ~100% CD47 receptor occupancy at RP2D
change_magnitude: ~100% CD47 receptor occupancy achieved at 30 mg/kg; phagocytosis mechanism inferred from preclinical + CD47 RO; no direct ex vivo phagocytosis assay quantified in abstract
durability_described: 91% ongoing responses at median follow-up 6.2-8.1 months
clinical_correlate: ORR 50%, CR 36%; FL 71% ORR, DLBCL 40% ORR
notes: Advani 2018 NEJM — landmark CD47 blockade + rituximab in rituximab-refractory NHL. Mechanism is macrophage phagocytosis restoration, but direct ex vivo phagocytosis assay not quantified in abstract. Anemia is on-target phagocytosis signal (depletion of senescent RBCs).
title: CD47 Blockade by Hu5F9-G4 and Rituximab in Non-Hodgkin's Lymphoma

tam_definition: Bone-marrow macrophage phagocytosis of CD47+ MDS blasts
change_magnitude: CR 33%, ORR 75%, mDOR 11.1 mo; mPFS 11.6 mo; TP53-mut CR 40%
durability_described: Median duration of CR 11.1 mo; mDOR 9.8 mo
clinical_correlate: CR 33%, ORR 75%, including 40% CR in TP53-mut
notes: Sallman 2023 JCO — magrolimab + aza in higher-risk MDS. CD47 blockade restores macrophage phagocytosis of MDS blasts (preclinical mechanism). Direct phagocytosis measurement not in abstract. Phase 3 ENHANCE (NCT04313881) subsequently failed (2024) — include for PD/efficacy context.
title: Magrolimab in Combination With Azacitidine in Patients With Higher-Risk Myelodysplastic Syndromes: Final Results of a Phase Ib Study

patient_population: Newly dx AML (n=54, median age 70) + R/R AML (n=52)
tam_definition: Bone-marrow macrophage-mediated phagocytosis of CD47+ AML blasts
change_magnitude: 1L CRc 63% (49% TP53-mut, 90% TP53 wt); R/R CRc 29%
clinical_correlate: 1L TP53-mut OS 7.6 mo vs wt OS 13 mo; R/R mOS 3.9 mo
notes: Daver 2025 Clin Cancer Res — aza+ven+magrolimab in AML. CD47-phagocytosis mechanism; no direct ex vivo phagocytosis in abstract. Context: broader magrolimab program halted after sepsis/anemia signals.
title: Azacitidine, Venetoclax, and Magrolimab in Newly Diagnosed and Relapsed Refractory Acute Myeloid Leukemia

dose: Evorpacept 30 or 60 mg/kg + lenalidomide 20 mg + rituximab 375 mg/m2
tam_definition: Tumor-infiltrating macrophages by spatial/IHC; anti-tumoral macrophage pathways by bulk RNA
change_magnitude: Significant increase in T cells and macrophages; anti-tumoral macrophage pathways upregulated
clinical_correlate: CR 80% (16/20); 2-yr PFS 69%; no DLTs
notes: Strati 2025 Clin Cancer Res — evorpacept + len + rituximab in R/R B-NHL. Rare example of intratumoral macrophage expansion toward anti-tumoral (M1-like) polarization. Assigned expansion-with-repolarization mechanism.
title: A Phase I Trial of Evorpacept, Lenalidomide, and Rituximab for Patients with B-Cell Non-Hodgkin Lymphoma

tissue_detail: Resected surgical specimen, post-treatment only (matched controls for comparison)
tam_definition: M2-like macrophages by IHC
change_magnitude: M2-like TAMs fewer in selicrelumab-treated tumors vs controls; tumor fibrosis reduced; intratumoral DCs more mature; T cells more active
clinical_correlate: Median OS 23.4 months (95% CI 18.0-28.8)
notes: Byrne 2021 Clin Cancer Res — neoadjuvant selicrelumab in resectable PDAC window-of-opportunity. M2-like TAM reduction + DC maturation + T-cell activation is coherent repolarization/ratio-shift signal. Abstract qualitative; full text has numeric IHC data.
title: Neoadjuvant Selicrelumab, an Agonist CD40 Antibody, Induces Changes in the Tumor Microenvironment in Patients with Resectable Pancreatic Cancer

tissue_detail: Paired pre/post-sotiga biopsies; scRNA-seq + CITEseq + MIBI
tam_definition: Myeloid cell clusters by scRNA-seq + CITEseq; spatial analysis by MIBI
change_magnitude: Increased antigen presentation; altered myeloid metabolism; elevated T-cell activation/cytotoxicity; reduced TME Tregs
clinical_correlate: pCR rate 38% (of treated patients)
notes: Soto 2024 Cancer Res Commun — neoadjuvant sotigalimab + CRT in E/GEJ cancer. Deep TIME-profiling via scRNA-seq + CITEseq + MIBI. Myeloid metabolism + antigen-presentation shift is a high-quality repolarization signal on multi-marker evidence.
title: Neoadjuvant CD40 Agonism Remodels the Tumor Immune Microenvironment in Locally Advanced Esophageal/Gastroesophageal Junction Cancer

tissue_detail: Peripheral cytokines and immune cell activation (pre-specified)
tam_definition: Innate and adaptive immune activation markers (cytokines + monocyte/DC activation)
change_magnitude: Dose-dependent cytokine induction; activation and trafficking of innate and adaptive immune cells
notes: Coveler 2023 JITC — SEA-CD40 phase 1. Non-fucosylated Fc increases ADCP by FcγR-enhanced binding. PD data is blood cytokine/immune-activation focused; no direct tumor TAM quantification in abstract.
title: SEA-CD40, a non-fucosylated CD40 agonist: a phase 1 dose-escalation study

tissue_detail: Injected tumor biopsies for immune infiltration analysis
tam_definition: Tumor-infiltrating immune cells (RNA expression + immunohistochemistry)
change_magnitude: Evidence of systemic immune activation; inflammatory cytokines elevated; T-cell clonal expansion; tumor biopsy RNA expression changes
clinical_correlate: 1 PR (Merkel cell), 2 uPR; lesion stable or decreased in 94% of injected lesions
notes: Meric-Bernstam 2022 Clin Cancer Res — MIW815 (ADU-S100) intratumoral STING phase 1. STING-driven type-I IFN mechanism is intended to repolarize myeloid TME; abstract documents immune activation but not direct TAM count/polarization. Full-text required for specific myeloid PD.
title: Phase I Dose-Escalation Trial of MIW815 (ADU-S100), an Intratumoral STING Agonist, in Patients with Advanced/Metastatic Solid Tumors or Lymphomas

patient_population: Heavily pre-treated solid tumors (melanoma, gastric, HCC, breast, biliary)
tissue_detail: Tumor biopsies pre/on-treatment; spatial transcriptomics + IHC
tam_definition: Intratumoral CLEVER-1+ macrophages; spatial transcriptomic macrophage activation signatures
change_magnitude: Macrophage activation signatures increase on-treatment; disease control correlated with high pre-treatment intratumoral CLEVER-1 positivity and increasing on-treatment serum IFNγ
clinical_correlate: DCR 25-40% across tumor types; no DLTs
notes: Rannikko 2023 Cell Rep Med — MATINS first-in-human bexmarilimab. The MATINS trial (Part I+II). Bexmarilimab converts immunosuppressive TAMs to activated phenotype; direct phagocytosis/function changes documented. Part I (n=30) reported separately in Virtakoivu 2021 (below).
title: Bexmarilimab-induced macrophage activation leads to treatment benefit in patients with solid tumors: The phase I/II first-in-human MATINS trial

tissue_detail: Blood monocytes (pre-specified as peripheral PD surrogate of macrophage reprogramming)
tam_definition: Peripheral blood monocytes by mass cytometry (pre-specified myeloid reprogramming PD)
change_magnitude: Blood monocytes exhibited proinflammatory phenotypic switch; suppressed nuclear lipid signaling; impaired vacuolar ATPase-mediated endosomal acidification; peripheral T-cell activation
notes: Virtakoivu 2021 Clin Cancer Res — bexmarilimab Part I (n=30) of MATINS. Peripheral myeloid reprogramming signal captured via CyTOF (D1 + D7 satisfied). Multi-dimensional evidence supports repolarization.
title: Systemic Blockade of Clever-1 Elicits Lymphocyte Activation Alongside Checkpoint Molecule Downregulation in Patients with Metastatic Cancer

dose: Bexmarilimab 1, 3, or 6 mg/kg IV weekly + aza standard
tam_definition: Bone-marrow macrophage reprogramming (CLEVER-1 target)
change_magnitude: ORR 45% (15/33); MTD not reached
clinical_correlate: ORR 45%; no DLTs; recommended dose 6 mg/kg for MDS
notes: Kontro 2025 Lancet Haematol — bexmarilimab + aza in MDS/AML phase 1. Marrow-macrophage reprogramming is the intended mechanism but direct marrow macrophage PD data not in abstract. Included for intervention coverage and the first hematologic CLEVER-1 trial.
title: Bexmarilimab plus azacitidine for high-risk myelodysplastic syndrome and relapsed or refractory acute myeloid leukaemia: a phase 1 trial

tissue_detail: Tumor biopsies — pharmacodynamic gene expression and TIS
tam_definition: Tumor inflammation signature + T-cell infiltration gene signature (LILRB2-blockade PD surrogate)
change_magnitude: PD gene expression changes reflecting increased tumor T-cell infiltration; baseline TIS predicts response
durability_described: One CR >2 years (Merkel cell carcinoma, monotherapy)
clinical_correlate: ORR 9% mono, 23% combo; 1 durable CR
notes: Taylor 2024 JITC — IO-108 anti-LILRB2 phase 1. Tumor transcriptomic PD (increased T-cell infiltration) is intended surrogate for myeloid reprogramming but direct macrophage cluster analysis not reported in abstract.
title: First-in-human phase I dose-escalation study of the anti-LILRB2 antibody IO-108 in patients with advanced solid tumors

tam_definition: TREM2+ tumor-associated macrophages (target depletion)
change_magnitude: Mechanism: PY314 'depletes tumor-associated macrophages'; abstract does not quantify tumor CD68/TREM2 change
clinical_correlate: Stable disease 50% (8/16); median PFS 2.69 mo
notes: Yeku 2025 JITC — PY159 and PY314 (TREM1 agonist + TREM2 antagonist) parallel arms in platinum-resistant ovarian. Abstract emphasizes mechanism and clinical outcomes; direct tumor TAM quantification not in abstract.
title: First-in-human trials of PY159 and PY314 myeloid targeting antibodies in platinum-resistant ovarian cancer

tam_definition: TREM1+ myeloid cells (reprogramming target)
change_magnitude: Mechanism: PY159 'reprograms immunosuppressive intratumoral myeloid cells'; abstract does not quantify
clinical_correlate: Stable disease 50% (8/17); median PFS 2.76 mo; TRAE 88.2%
notes: Yeku 2025 JITC PY159 arm. Paired with PY314 row above. Classified as other-repolarizing since PY159 is a myeloid-reprogramming agonist not fitting the 12 canonical families exactly.
title: First-in-human trials of PY159 and PY314 myeloid targeting antibodies in platinum-resistant ovarian cancer

tam_definition: TREM2+ macrophages (target depletion)
change_magnitude: mPFS 1.4 mo; 1 PR, 4 SD
clinical_correlate: Limited anti-tumor effect in CPI-refractory RCC
notes: Beckermann 2024 Invest New Drugs — PY314 + pembro in CPI-refractory RCC. Abstract is PK/safety focused; no TAM PD magnitude. Clinical activity poor — TREM2 targeting does not overcome prior CPI resistance in RCC.
title: A phase 1b open-label study to evaluate the safety, tolerability, pharmacokinetics, and pharmacodynamics of PY314 in combination with pembrolizumab in patients with advanced renal cell carcinoma

tam_definition: CD68+CD163+ M2-like tumor-associated macrophages by IHC
change_magnitude: Decreased CD68+CD163+ macrophages in tumor biopsies of 9/28 patients (32%); CD8+ T-cell increase in 11/28 (39%); composite positive immune response in up to 57%
clinical_correlate: Positive immunological response correlates with improved PFS
notes: Sun 2024 TARMIC Mol Cancer — metronomic trabectedin + cyclo in STS. 32% of patients show tumor CD68+CD163+ reduction — a partial-response signal. Compelling ratio-shift (9/28 TAM ↓ with 11/28 CD8 ↑) and PFS correlate.
title: Impact of metronomic trabectedin and cyclophosphamide on the tumor microenvironment in refractory soft-tissue sarcoma patients (TARMIC study)

patient_population: Treated STS patients (clinical component of mechanistic paper)
tissue_detail: Paired tumor biopsies in treated sarcoma patients
tam_definition: CD68+ macrophages by IHC; monocyte depletion in blood
change_magnitude: Monocyte depletion (including TAMs) observed in treated patients
notes: Germano 2013 Cancer Cell — landmark paper establishing trabectedin's selective macrophage depletion mechanism. Includes small human/clinical sidecar (small N, ~5 paired biopsies per the preclinical+translational methodology). Low N for clinical row (borderline for N≥3 rule); retained as foundational. Full-text would confirm exact N and tissue-compartment magnitudes.
title: Role of macrophage targeting in the antitumor activity of trabectedin

tam_definition: Tumor immune infiltrate (CD4/CD8 T cells primary; immune-gene activation)
change_magnitude: Increased inflammation in injected tumors; CD8+ and CD4+ T-cell infiltration; activation of immune-related genes
durability_described: 2 patients recurrence-free at 44+/41+ months; 2 sustained PRs at 33+ months
clinical_correlate: Sustained PRs in metastatic cohort
notes: Bhatia 2019 Clin Cancer Res — intratumoral G100 in Merkel cell carcinoma phase 1. TLR4 agonism intended to repolarize myeloid TME; abstract-level data are T-cell and gene-expression focused with no direct TAM CD68/CD163 quantification. depletion_success: not-assessed due to no direct macrophage readout.
title: Intratumoral G100, a TLR4 Agonist, Induces Antitumor Immune Responses and Tumor Regression in Patients with Merkel Cell Carcinoma
